name: Two Six
description: The Two Six (Gangster Two Six) are a Latino (Mexican) Folk Nation street gang founded circa 1970 in Little Village (South Lawndale), Chicago. Started as 'Twenty Sixth Street Boys' — a group of teens at 26th and Sawyer who hung out at a Jack in the Box, named after the street Latin Kings called them by. The Ayala family transformed them into a major gang in 1977, establishing colors (black and beige from khaki pants at the Alfonso and Elias store) and connections to Mexican drug cartels through the Montemayor family. Leader David 'El Jefe' Ayala ran the gang from prison until 1996. Grew to nearly 10,000 members with sections across Chicago's southwest side and suburbs including Cicero, Berwyn, and Joliet.
